ops.title: Guides check warns on drift; captain-hook review dispatch is native
ops.body:
Decisions 2026-07-16 (user), after captain-hook artifact drift reddened unrelated pushes with 'stale rendered guides':

1. WARN, don't fail: the Guides check action (yasyf/cc-guides@action-v1) treats check exit 1 (drift) as a non-blocking ::warning:: + step summary — the daily re-render heals within 24h; exit >=2 (missing/dev/local lock, layout-lock mismatch) still fails. No push-triggered self-heal, no fleet stub sweep; the manual action-v1 tag move (now action-v1.1.0 @ fe6b3ad in cc-guides) is the entire rollout. Verified: failed run 29475011381 rerun green with the warning.
2. Native review dispatch (capt-hook v9.23.0, full migration per user 'all capt-hook stuff should be registered hooks'): async run SessionStart/SessionEnd fires the enrollment-gated reviewer, run Stop the throttled sweep, via dispatch_review() in review/pipeline.py; the raw 'capt-hook review run/sweep' entries are GONE from the shipped plugin hooks.json AND captain-hook's dogfood settings overlay. Guards from adversarial review: atomic O_EXCL stamp claims (version-skew raw+native double-fire collapses to one child), enrollment gate BEFORE the claim (a gated skip can't burn the stamp the raw fallback needs), enrolled() opens the store busy_timeout=0 and FAILS OPEN (child is the authoritative gate; a held writer lock had silently dropped reviews for 13s in review). Proven by live dispatch matrix in an isolated CAPTAIN_HOOK_STATE_DIR.
3. Drift backport: both drifted edits (f0aa0551 AGENTS.md, 48196507 settings sweep line) were captain-hook-specific — ported to the repo-local fragments (dev-guide fragment paragraph rewritten; sweep line superseded by native dispatch). Repo-level override support already existed (JSON overlay deep-merge; md local-fragment shadowing, documented in cc-guides README 'Keep one repo's spin on a shared guide') — no new cc-guides feature.
4. Prevention (in flight): rendered-artifact guard ships as a capt-hook pack via a new cc-guides plugin/marketplace, wired fleet-wide through settings-base; cc-notes was NEVER migrated to the attach contract — getaway/cc-context/cc-present are the references.
